properties.remark: Roswell Relief Route between Sunset and Brasher closed due to low visibility in blowing dust.
Spotter reported a snow squall moving through her residence. She reported very strong north winds with visibility under a quarter of a mile as the snow squall was moving through.
Whiteout conditions from blowing snow along a snow squall moving south through Quay County.
Video on social media showed a brief and weak landspout near 98th Street and I-40. Damage extent unknown.
Multiple vehicle crash, including semi-tucks, on US 285 south of Roswell due to near-zero visibility in blowing dust.
Video from trained spotter shows near-zero visibility in blowing dust. Reports of a multi-vehicle accident.
US 285 from Roswell to Artesia remains closed due to near zero visibility in blowing dust. State Road 2 has also been closed. Several multiple vehicle accidents have been reported by Law Enforcement.
Roswell Relief Route between Sunset and McGaffey St closed due to zero visibility. Road remained closed until 556pm.
U.S. Highway 285 closed between mm 79 and 99 (south of Midway to north of Artesia) due to zero visibility. Road remained closed until 215 pm.

Highway 285 at mm 105 closed due to zero visibility. Road remained closed until 215pm.

Overall rating is EF-1 with 90 mph windspeeds based on what damage indicators there were from power pole damage as no structures were impacted. Tornado tracked from 3mi SSW of Floyd to 2mi ENE of Floyd from 520PM to 537PM MDT. Peak intensity of the tornado moved over NM road 267 snapping 15-20 power poles. This LSR corrects the previous tornado LSR on May 25.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 3 NNW Forrest. Storm survey determined that flipped over 18 wheeler trailer was caused by high winds along a gust front ahead of a line of storms. *** 1 FATAL *** DELAYED REPORT: Albuquerque Fire Rescue received reports of a man in the arroyo near Carlisle Boulevard and Menaul Boulevard. Crews did not spot the man after one hour of searching at multiple locations searching the north diversion channel arroyo. An airboat was deployed by the Bernalillo County Fire Department later Thursday morning in the washout area at Roy Avenue and Fourth Street. No one was found. The individual is assumed to have deceased.

*** 3 FATAL, 2 INJ *** DELAYED REPORT: 3 people, including 2 children, died in floodwaters from catastrophic flash flooding on the South Fork burn scar. The 2 children, a 7 year old male and a 4 year old female, died after floodwaters engulfed and destroyed the family RV. The parents of these children suffered serious injuries and were hospitalized due to trying to save their children from the floodwaters. Two family dogs were also lost in the floodwaters. Additionally, a 40 to 50 year old male died after being swept downstream in the record high flood waters.
